acceptance.proof: An external consumer resolves representative accepted names through both unchanged root paths and additive role/plane paths with exact type identity.
Namespaces contain re-exports only, name contract/runtime consumer roles, and private sibling implementation modules remain private.
The accepted feature lock/fingerprint and runtime implementation sources are unchanged from LSLC-003F while the complete owner suite passes.
No new public type, runtime authority, dependency, feature effect, device scope, adapter, or Manifold claim is added.

commit_policy: Commit proposal and lifecycle separately, then one additive namespace/test/documentation family, validation, acceptance, and no-force publication evidence.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the additive public role/plane facade and its invariants.
Show supported root compatibility and optional consumer namespaces.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Compile the external public API consumer.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Record facade-only dependency direction and single implementation authority.
Review lane, module-layout, and public-boundary routing; current guidance already requires facade preservation and single authority.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed installed skill and pinned workflow documents.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review facade preservation, contract/runtime role separation, dependency direction, and single authority; current guidance supports re-export-only consumer projections.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed installed skill and contracts/modules/activation reference.

non_scope: Moving, renaming, or exposing private implementation modules; changing sibling implementation imports or defining a second authority.

Activation, feature descriptors or lock, runtime behavior, protocol bytes, error stages, limits, cancellation, cleanup, dependencies, unsafe/FFI, devices, official-source intake, adapters, or Manifold authority.
First-hop documentation compaction, Clippy policy, String, Int64, chunks, multi-channel, or broader discovery.
objective: Add the smallest additive contract/runtime consumer namespaces over the accepted public API, retain every root re-export as the compatibility facade, and prove the supported surface from an external integration test without changing implementation ownership or runtime behavior.
outputs: Public contract and runtime namespaces group existing root-exported names by consumer role without defining new types or authority.
Every accepted root public name remains available unchanged.
An external integration test imports only rusty_lsl public paths and proves root/namespace type identity plus inert contract construction.
